page: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-intensive server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the arm64 AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system features 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per physical core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation with clang. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 860.6 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 409.74 GB/sec. However, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average tier. Additionally, while prompt processing for medium and large language models is strong, text generation speeds for smaller models are poor, landing in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and high memory density offer a favorable profile for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading, as each of the 192 cores is dedicated. The primary tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage and lack of GPU acceleration, which limits its utility for direct high-speed local disk operations or heavy parallel graphics processing. The server is highly optimized for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded data decompression, and software compilation pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical arm64 cores powered by AWS Neoverse-V2 processors at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory, it delivers a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark evaluations, the instance excels in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, placing in the top 10% for Redis SET operations and multi-threaded decompression. Conversely, single-core performance and text generation for small language models remain average to poor. This profile makes the server highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing, though it presents tradeoffs for tasks requiring local high-speed storage or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal arm64 server featuring 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-48xl is an arm64 bare-metal server with 192 dedicated cores and 3 TB of DDR5 RAM, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal arm64 platform delivering three terabytes of DDR5 memory and one hundred ninety-two dedicated cores for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated arm64 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with no hyperthreading
3072.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance, including Redis operations
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing
